system_prompt:
You convert one plain-language browsing task into a NetGent workflow.

A NetGent workflow is a JSON array of STATES. NetGent runs like a non-deterministic finite
automaton: on every page it checks each state's TRIGGERS, and when one matches it runs that
state's ACTIONS. So each state must be recognizable from the page it applies to.

Rules for a good workflow:
1. Each state is an object with keys: "name", "description", "triggers", "actions".
   The FINAL state must also include "end_state" (a short completion string).
2. TRIGGERS describe how to recognize the page the state applies to. Prefer stable signals:
   a URL substring, a visible unique text/placeholder/button label. Give 1-2 triggers per state.
   Do NOT rely on brittle exact URLs for pages that change (e.g. article slugs) -- use a
   substring or a visible element instead.
3. ACTIONS are concrete, ordered, one instruction each: navigate, click X, type 'Y' into Z,
   press Enter, scroll down N pixels, etc. Keep them specific enough to execute without guessing.
4. First state should start from a fresh tab (trigger on chrome://new-tab-page/ ) and navigate
   to the starting site, unless the sentence implies you are already somewhere.
5. The last state confirms the goal was reached and terminates with an "end_state".
6. Keep it minimal: one state per meaningful page transition. Don't invent steps the sentence
   didn't ask for.
7. A state's "actions" list MAY be empty ([]) if the state exists purely to wait/observe --
   e.g. "waiting for email verification to complete" or "waiting for a redirect after login."
   NetGent will just keep re-checking that state's triggers on every poll until a DIFFERENT
   state's trigger matches (such as the page after verification succeeds), so an empty-action
   state is how you express "sit here until something external changes." Use this sparingly --
   only for genuine external waits (2FA, email confirmation links, redirects) -- and always add
   the NEXT state that recognizes what the page looks like once the wait is over.

Output ONLY a single JSON array and nothing else. It MUST be strictly valid JSON:
  - No markdown fences, no prose before or after.
  - NO comments of any kind (no // ... and no /* ... */).
  - No trailing commas.
  - Every string in double quotes; use single quotes for literals inside a string
    (e.g. "Type 'hello' into the box").
user_prompt:
User sentence:
"go to wikipedia, search for bezier curves, and scroll all the way down to the end of the article"

Workflow:
[
  {
    "name": "On Browser Home Page",
    "description": "Start the process from a fresh browser tab.",
    "triggers": ["If the current URL is chrome://new-tab-page/"],
    "actions": ["Navigate to https://en.wikipedia.org/"]
  },
  {
    "name": "On Wikipedia Homepage",
    "description": "Search for a specific topic on Wikipedia.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL contains en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page",
      "If a search box with placeholder 'Search Wikipedia' is visible"
    ],
    "actions": [
      "Click the search box",
      "Type 'Bezier curve' into the search box",
      "Press Enter"
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "On Search Results Or Article Page",
    "description": "Confirm the search navigated to the Bezier curve article page.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL contains en.wikipedia.org/wiki/B",
      "If the current URL contains en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:Search"
    ],
    "actions": [
      "If on a search results list, click the first search result link",
      "Scroll down 600 pixels on the article page"
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "Article Loaded And Scrolled",
    "description": "The target article is open and has been scrolled, confirming successful load.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL contains en.wikipedia.org/wiki/",
      "If the page has scrolled away from the top"
    ],
    "actions": ["Terminate: task complete, article reached and scrolled"],
    "end_state": "Action completed"
  }
]

system_prompt:
A NetGent workflow you generated did not finish. NetGent stalled or failed before reaching the
final end_state. Your job is to produce a BETTER, MORE SPECIFIC version of the workflow that is
more likely to get past the point where it stalled.

Most stalls are caused by a trigger that never matched (too strict / wrong URL / element not
present) or an action that was too vague. Fix the workflow by:
  - Loosening or correcting the trigger of the state where it got stuck (use a URL substring or
    a visible text/label instead of an exact match), and/or adding an alternative trigger.
  - Making the stuck state's actions more explicit and granular (e.g. split "search for X" into
    click search box -> type X -> press Enter; add an intermediate "click first result" step).
  - Adding a recovery/intermediate state if a page in between was missed (cookie banner, consent
    dialog, redirect, results page before the article).
Reminder: a state's "actions" can legitimately be [] if it's a pure wait/observe state for
something external (2FA, email verification, a redirect) -- that's fine and not a bug, as long
as a later state recognizes the page once the wait resolves.
Keep the parts that already worked. Do NOT add explanatory notes or caveats as comments -- if you
must note an assumption, fold it into a state's "description" field instead. Return the full
corrected workflow as one JSON array, same format as before (final state keeps its "end_state").

Output ONLY a single JSON array and nothing else. It MUST be strictly valid JSON:
  - No markdown fences, no prose before or after.
  - NO comments of any kind (no // ... and no /* ... */).
  - No trailing commas.
  - Every string in double quotes; use single quotes for literals inside a string
    (e.g. "Type 'hello' into the box").
user_prompt:
Original task:
"I want you to go to the paper with this title: "NetGent: Agent-Based Automation of Network Application Workflows", find the citations at the bottom of the paper, and then follow the link to the paper about human imitation."

Workflow that failed:
[
  {
    "name": "Start Browser and Go to Google Scholar",
    "description": "Opens a new browser tab and navigates to Google Scholar to begin searching for the paper.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL is chrome://new-tab-page/"
    ],
    "actions": [
      "Navigate to https://scholar.google.com/"
    ],
    "end_state": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Search for the Target Paper",
    "description": "Enters the full title of the specified paper into the Google Scholar search bar and initiates the search.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL contains scholar.google.com",
      "If a search input with name 'q' is visible"
    ],
    "actions": [
      "Type 'NetGent: Agent-Based Automation of Network Application Workflows' into the input with name 'q'",
      "Press Enter"
    ],
    "end_state": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Click Target Paper from Search Results",
    "description": "Locates and clicks the search result link that exactly matches the paper's title.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL contains scholar.google.com/scholar",
      "If a link with text 'NetGent: Agent-Based Automation of Network Application Workflows' is visible"
    ],
    "actions": [
      "Click the link with text 'NetGent: Agent-Based Automation of Network Application Workflows'"
    ],
    "end_state": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Scroll to Citations Section",
    "description": "After navigating to the paper's page, scrolls down to reveal the references or citations section.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL does not contain scholar.google.com",
      "If text 'NetGent: Agent-Based Automation of Network Application Workflows' is visible"
    ],
    "actions": [
      "Scroll down 1500 pixels"
    ],
    "end_state": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Follow 'Human Imitation' Citation",
    "description": "Identifies and clicks the citation link related to 'human imitation' within the references section of the paper.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL does not contain scholar.google.com",
      "If text 'NetGent: Agent-Based Automation of Network Application Workflows' is visible",
      "If a link containing 'human imitation' is visible"
    ],
    "actions": [
      "Click the link containing 'human imitation'"
    ],
    "end_state": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Arrived at Human Imitation Paper",
    "description": "Confirms successful navigation to the paper about human imitation.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL does not contain 'NetGent: Agent-Based Automation of Network Application Workflows'",
      "If text 'human imitation' is visible"
    ],
    "actions": [
      "Terminate: Successfully navigated to the paper about human imitation"
    ],
    "end_state": "Human imitation paper reached"
  }
]

FAILURE CLASS: TIMEOUT
What the verifier observed: Passed 5 state(s) (Start Browser and Go to Google Scholar, Search for the Target Paper, Click Target Paper from Search Results, Scroll to Citations Section, Follow 'Human Imitation' Citation) but never reached the terminal state 'Arrived at Human Imitation Paper'.
State where it got stuck: Follow 'Human Imitation' Citation
States that ran successfully first: Start Browser and Go to Google Scholar, Search for the Target Paper, Click Target Paper from Search Results, Scroll to Citations Section, Follow 'Human Imitation' Citation
Evidence:
  (none provided)

HOW TO FIX THIS SPECIFIC FAILURE:
The workflow sat on a state without advancing. Either the trigger is matching a page it shouldn't, or the following state's trigger is too strict to fire. Tighten the stuck state's trigger and loosen the NEXT state's trigger.

Produce the corrected workflow now.

Original task:
"I want you to go to the paper with this title: "NetGent: Agent-Based Automation of Network Application Workflows", find the citations at the bottom of the paper, and then follow the link to the paper about human imitation."

Workflow that failed:
[
  {
    "name": "Start Browser and Go to Google Scholar",
    "description": "Opens a new browser tab and navigates to Google Scholar to begin searching for the paper.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL is chrome://new-tab-page/"
    ],
    "actions": [
      "Navigate to https://scholar.google.com/"
    ],
    "end_state": ""
  },
  {

    "description": "Enters the full title of the specified paper into the Google Scholar search bar and initiates the search.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL contains scholar.google.com",
      "If a search input with name 'q' is visible"
    ],
    "actions": [
      "Type 'NetGent: Agent-Based Automation of Network Application Workflows' into the input with name 'q'",
      "Press Enter"
    ],
    "end_state": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Click Target Paper from Search Results",
    "description": "Locates and clicks the search result link that contains the paper's title, allowing for minor variations.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL contains scholar.google.com/scholar",
      "If a link with text contains 'NetGent: Agent-Based Automation of Network Application Workflows' is visible"
    ],
    "actions": [
      "Click the link with text contains 'NetGent: Agent-Based Automation of Network Application Workflows'"
    ],
    "end_state": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Scroll to Citations Section",
    "description": "After navigating to the paper's page, scrolls down to reveal the references or citations section. This assumes the paper page is loaded and not on Google Scholar.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL does not contain scholar.google.com",
      "If text 'NetGent: Agent-Based Automation of Network Application Workflows' is visible"
    ],
    "actions": [
      "Scroll down 1500 pixels"
    ],
    "end_state": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Follow 'Human Imitation' Citation",
    "description": "Identifies and clicks the citation link related to 'human imitation' within the references section of the paper. This state assumes the NetGent paper page is loaded and the citations are visible.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL does not contain scholar.google.com",
      "If text 'NetGent: Agent-Based Automation of Network Application Workflows' is visible",
      "If a link containing 'human imitation' is visible"
    ],
    "actions": [
      "Click the link containing 'human imitation'"
    ],
    "end_state": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Arrived at Human Imitation Paper",
    "description": "Confirms successful navigation to the paper about human imitation. Triggers are broadened to account for various landing page types (publisher page, PDF, etc.) and less strict text matching, checking page title, URL, and visible text for keywords.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL does not contain 'NetGent: Agent-Based Automation of Network Application Workflows'",
      "If the page title contains 'imitation'",
      "If the page title contains 'mimicry'",
      "If the page title contains 'human'",
      "If the current URL contains 'pdf'",
      "If text contains 'imitation'",
      "If text contains 'mimicry'",
      "If text contains 'human'"
    ],
    "actions": [
      "Terminate: Successfully navigated to the paper about human imitation"
    ],
    "end_state": "Human imitation paper reached"
  }
]

FAILURE CLASS: EMPTY_TRIGGERS
What the verifier observed: State(s) [Start Browser and Go to Google Scholar] were cached with an empty checks list, so they matched every page and the workflow never advanced.
State where it got stuck: Start Browser and Go to Google Scholar
States that ran successfully first: Start Browser and Go to Google Scholar
Evidence:
  (none provided)

HOW TO FIX THIS SPECIFIC FAILURE:
The stuck state was cached with an EMPTY trigger list, which makes NetGent match it against every page and loop forever. Give this state at least one CONCRETE, synthesizable trigger: a URL substring, or a specific visible text string. Never emit a state with an empty triggers list.

Produce the corrected workflow now.
raw_output:
```json
[
  {
    "name": "Start Browser and Go to Google Scholar",
    "description": "Opens a new browser tab and navigates to Google Scholar to begin searching for the paper. This state is triggered when a new, blank tab is opened.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL is chrome://new-tab-page/",
      "If the current URL is about:blank"
    ],
    "actions": [
      "Navigate to https://scholar.google.com/"
    ],
    "end_state": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Search for the Target Paper",
    "description": "Enters the full title of the specified paper into the Google Scholar search bar and initiates the search.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL contains scholar.google.com",
      "If a search input with name 'q' is visible"
    ],
    "actions": [
      "Type 'NetGent: Agent-Based Automation of Network Application Workflows' into the input with name 'q'",
      "Press Enter"
    ],
    "end_state": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Click Target Paper from Search Results",
    "description": "Locates and clicks the search result link that contains the paper's title, allowing for minor variations. This state expects to be on the Google Scholar search results page.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL contains scholar.google.com/scholar",
      "If a link with text contains 'NetGent: Agent-Based Automation of Network Application Workflows' is visible"
    ],
    "actions": [
      "Click the link with text contains 'NetGent: Agent-Based Automation of Network Application Workflows'"
    ],
    "end_state": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Scroll to Citations Section",
    "description": "After navigating to the paper's page (off Google Scholar), scrolls down to reveal the references or citations section. This assumes the paper page is loaded and the target paper's title is visible.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL does not contain scholar.google.com",
      "If text 'NetGent: Agent-Based Automation of Network Application Workflows' is visible"
    ],
    "actions": [
      "Scroll down 1500 pixels"
    ],
    "end_state": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Follow 'Human Imitation' Citation",
    "description": "Identifies and clicks the citation link related to 'human imitation' within the references section of the paper. This state assumes the NetGent paper page is loaded, the citations are visible, and the link is present.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL does not contain scholar.google.com",
      "If text 'NetGent: Agent-Based Automation of Network Application Workflows' is visible",
      "If a link containing 'human imitation' is visible"
    ],
    "actions": [
      "Click the link containing 'human imitation'"
    ],
    "end_state": ""
  },
  {
    "name": "Arrived at Human Imitation Paper",
    "description": "Confirms successful navigation to the paper about human imitation. Triggers are broadened to account for various landing page types (publisher page, PDF, etc.) and less strict text matching, checking page title, URL, and visible text for keywords.",
    "triggers": [
      "If the current URL does not contain 'NetGent: Agent-Based Automation of Network Application Workflows'",
      "If the page title contains 'imitation'",
      "If the page title contains 'mimicry'",
      "If the page title contains 'human'",
      "If the current URL contains 'pdf'",
      "If text contains 'imitation'",
      "If text contains 'mimicry'",
      "If text contains 'human'"
    ],
    "actions": [
      "Terminate: Successfully navigated to the paper about human imitation"
    ],
    "end_state": "Human imitation paper reached"
  }
]
```
